content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms like ICM can be repurposed to dynamically allocate an autonomous agent's limited working memory capacity: agents prioritize holding unexpected, unresolved information in working memory while rapidly discarding predictable, low-information content to avoid capacity overload during reasoning. This creates a natural memory bottleneck that mirrors human cognitive limitation, which actually improves long-run out-of-distribution generalization by forcing agents to only retain high-curiosity, high-impact state representations.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
